channel.title: Crimp Weekly
channel.description: Fresh takes for indoor boulderers and new climbers.

item.title: Ask the forge: your recovery questions, answered
item.description: Straight answers to the recovery questions indoor boulderers and new climbers keep asking, minus the hedging.

item.title: The quiet upgrade: small tweaks in gear with outsized returns
item.description: No purchases, no overhauls — the modest gear adjustments that indoor boulderers and new climbers report mattering most.

item.title: Myth vs. reality: climbing shoe guide
item.description: The persistent myths around climbing shoe guide, stress-tested against what indoor boulderers and new climbers actually report.

item.title: A weekend project: level up your technique in two hours
item.description: One contained, satisfying technique project for indoor boulderers — start Saturday morning, done by lunch.

item.title: Community pulse: what r/bouldering is debating this week
item.description: The arguments, consensus shifts, and quietly useful threads from the recovery community this week.

item.title: The gear checklist: run this before you spend
item.description: Seven questions that separate a justified gear purchase from an expensive mood.

item.title: Five mistakes everyone makes in their first month of training
item.description: The most common early stumbles in training, and the cheap corrections that fix them for indoor boulderers and new climbers.

item.title: "bouldering technique for beginners" in practice: a real-world field test
item.description: We put the most-repeated advice about bouldering technique for beginners through a month of actual use. Some of it survived.

item.title: Ask the forge: your recovery questions, answered
item.description: Straight answers to the recovery questions indoor boulderers and new climbers keep asking, minus the hedging.

item.title: The quiet upgrade: small tweaks in gear with outsized returns
item.description: No purchases, no overhauls — the modest gear adjustments that indoor boulderers and new climbers report mattering most.

item.title: Myth vs. reality: bouldering technique for beginners
item.description: The persistent myths around bouldering technique for beginners, stress-tested against what indoor boulderers and new climbers actually report.

item.title: A weekend project: level up your technique in two hours
item.description: One contained, satisfying technique project for indoor boulderers — start Saturday morning, done by lunch.

item.title: Community pulse: what r/climbharder is debating this week
item.description: The arguments, consensus shifts, and quietly useful threads from the recovery community this week.

item.title: The gear checklist: run this before you spend
item.description: Seven questions that separate a justified gear purchase from an expensive mood.

item.title: Five mistakes everyone makes in their first month of training
item.description: The most common early stumbles in training, and the cheap corrections that fix them for indoor boulderers and new climbers.

item.title: "hangboard routine" in practice: a real-world field test
item.description: We put the most-repeated advice about hangboard routine through a month of actual use. Some of it survived.

item.title: Ask the forge: your recovery questions, answered
item.description: Straight answers to the recovery questions indoor boulderers and new climbers keep asking, minus the hedging.

item.title: The quiet upgrade: small tweaks in gear with outsized returns
item.description: No purchases, no overhauls — the modest gear adjustments that indoor boulderers and new climbers report mattering most.

item.title: Myth vs. reality: hangboard routine
item.description: The persistent myths around hangboard routine, stress-tested against what indoor boulderers and new climbers actually report.

item.title: A weekend project: level up your technique in two hours
item.description: One contained, satisfying technique project for indoor boulderers — start Saturday morning, done by lunch.

item.title: Community pulse: what r/climbing is debating this week
item.description: The arguments, consensus shifts, and quietly useful threads from the recovery community this week.

item.title: The state of Technique in 2026
item.description: A field report on technique: where things stand, and what indoor boulderers and new climbers should do next.

item.title: Starter guide: Training without the overwhelm
item.description: A practical on-ramp to training for indoor boulderers and new climbers — one repeatable workflow, no gear debt.

item.title: "bouldering technique for beginners" — separating signal from noise
item.description: Thousands of people search "bouldering technique for beginners" every month. Most of what they find is recycled. Here is what holds up.

item.title: Why indoor boulderers are rethinking Gear
item.description: A quiet shift is underway in how indoor boulderers and new climbers approach gear — less maximalism, more maintenance.

item.title: Recovery on a budget: what actually matters
item.description: Where money changes outcomes in recovery — and the line items that are pure vanity.

item.title: What to watch next in Training: five quiet shifts
item.description: Ignore the headlines — these are the slower currents reshaping training for indoor boulderers and new climbers.
